basic_details.job_title: CSIR CECRI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Walk in for 27 Posts
basic_details.application_mode: Walk-in Interview (with prior online apprenticeship portal registration)
basic_details.short_information: CSIR-Central Electrochemical Research Institute (CSIR-CECRI), Karaikudi - a premier institute functioning under the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), Department of Scientific & Industrial Research (DSIR), Ministry of Science & Technology - has invited applications for a walk-in interview to engage 27 Apprentice Trainees across Trade (ITI), Technician (Diploma), and Graduate (Degree) categories. The engagement is to impart training under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961 (as amended). The walk-in interview will be held on 10 August 2026 (Monday) at CSIR-CECRI, Karaikudi.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on the date of the interview, i.e., 10 August 2026. | Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules for reserved categories. Ex-servicemen: The upper age limit is relaxed by an additional 10 years, to the extent of service rendered in the Defence Forces.

qualification.details: Candidates must have passed the particular trade (except Glass Former and Processor) of the NCVT pattern designated by the Director General of Employment & Training (D.G.E.&T). Candidates must be registered as Trade Apprentices in the NAPS portal before the date of the walk-in interview. | A full-time graduate degree granted by a recognized university/institution in the relevant discipline. A full-time Diploma in Engineering/Technology granted by a State Council or Board of Technical Education established by a State Government/University/Institution in the relevant discipline. Graduates/Technicians who passed their qualifying examination in 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 are eligible to apply. | Candidates holding any Post Graduation qualification (including an integrated PG course). Candidates who passed from institutions not approved by AICTE/DOTE/UGC. Candidates who have already undergone, or are currently pursuing, apprenticeship training in the same qualification as per the Apprenticeship Act, 1961. Candidates with one year or more of work experience in any organization after acquiring the essential qualification. Sandwich diplomas, private diplomas, and part-time/distance/short-term/certificate courses are not acceptable.

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification: Passed 8th standard from a recognized school

discipline_wise_breakdown.essential_qualification: Passed 8th standard from a recognized school

salary_details.details: Stipend ranges from Rs. 6,800 to Rs. 12,300 per month depending on the trade, discipline, and category, as detailed in the vacancy tables above. All positions carry a training period of 1 year.

selection_process: Selection is through a walk-in interview at CSIR-CECRI
Karaikudi. If the number of candidates is higher than the available vacancies
the Selection Committee will adopt its own criteria to shortlist candidates for the walk-in interview
in proportion to the available vacancies. The applicant's candidature is provisional and subject to subsequent verification of certificates and testimonials.
how_to_apply: Download the application form from the "Opportunities" page of the CSIR-CECRI official website: https://cecri.res.in/

Register on the appropriate apprenticeship portal based on your qualification category: ITI (Trade) Apprentices: https://apprenticeshipindia.org/candidate-registration Graduate (Degree)/Technician (Diploma) Apprentices: https://nats.education.gov.in/
Fill in all details in the application form, including the Enrolment Number generated after successful registration.
Bring the duly filled-in application form, along with all original certificates and one set of self-attested photocopies of relevant testimonials, on the date of the interview.
Report to CSIR-CECRI, Karaikudi by 08:45 AM on 10 August 2026. Entry will not be permitted after 10:30 AM.

_labels.notification_pdf: Download CSIR-CECRI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

all_official_links.label: Download CSIR-CECRI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Quick Overview

rows.details: Walk-in Interview (with prior online apprenticeship portal registration)

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates
CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Vacancy 2026 Details
content_sections.text: Total Trade (ITI) Positions: 18 Total Technician (Diploma) Positions: 5 Total Graduate Positions: 4 Grand Total: 27 Apprentice Trainee Positions Note: Candidates appearing for the walk-in interview for apprenticeship training in any trade/discipline other than those listed above will not be entertained.

rows.essential_qualification: Passed 8th standard from a recognized school

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must have passed the particular trade (except Glass Former and Processor) of the NCVT pattern designated by the Director General of Employment & Training (D.G.E.&T). Candidates must be registered as Trade Apprentices in the NAPS portal before the date of the walk-in interview. A full-time graduate degree granted by a recognized university/institution in the relevant discipline. A full-time Diploma in Engineering/Technology granted by a State Council or Board of Technical Education established by a State Government/University/Institution in the relevant discipline. Graduates/Technicians who passed their qualifying examination in 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 are eligible to apply. Candidates holding any Post Graduation qualification (including an integrated PG course). Candidates who passed from institutions not approved by AICTE/DOTE/UGC. Candidates who have already undergone, or are currently pursuing, apprenticeship training in the same qualification as per the Apprenticeship Act, 1961. Candidates with one year or more of work experience in any organization after acquiring the essential qualification. Sandwich diplomas, private diplomas, and part-time/distance/short-term/certificate courses are not acceptable.

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Age Limit 2026
content_sections.text: Age is calculated as on the date of the interview, i.e., 10 August 2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules for reserved categories. Ex-servicemen: The upper age limit is relaxed by an additional 10 years, to the extent of service rendered in the Defence Forces.

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Stipend 2026
content_sections.text: Stipend ranges from Rs. 6,800 to Rs. 12,300 per month depending on the trade, discipline, and category, as detailed in the vacancy tables above. All positions carry a training period of 1 year.

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Reservation of Vacancies
content_sections.text: Guidelines under the Apprentices Act on reservation for SC/ST/OBC/PWD/EWS will be followed. Candidates claiming reservation under SC/ST/OBC/PWD/EWS must provide a certificate as per the Government of India format; failing this, their claim will be considered under the General category only.

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is through a walk-in interview at CSIR-CECRI, Karaikudi. If the number of candidates is higher than the available vacancies, the Selection Committee will adopt its own criteria to shortlist candidates for the walk-in interview, in proportion to the available vacancies. The applicant's candidature is provisional and subject to subsequent verification of certificates and testimonials.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Walk-in Interview
content_sections.text: Duly filled-in application form (downloaded from the CECRI website) Enrolment Number generated after successful registration on the NAPS/NATS portal All original certificates/testimonials related to qualification and date of birth One set of self-attested photocopies of all relevant testimonials Caste certificate (for SC/ST/OBC Non-Creamy Layer/EWS candidates, as applicable) in the prescribed Government of India format One recent passport-size photograph If the date of result declaration is not mentioned in the mark sheet, a certificate from the Principal of the Polytechnic/College/Institute stating the date of publication of the result

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Download the application form from the "Opportunities" page of the CSIR-CECRI official website: https://cecri.res.in/ Register on the appropriate apprenticeship portal based on your qualification category: ITI (Trade) Apprentices: https://apprenticeshipindia.org/candidate-registration Graduate (Degree)/Technician (Diploma) Apprentices: https://nats.education.gov.in/ Fill in all details in the application form, including the Enrolment Number generated after successful registration. Bring the duly filled-in application form, along with all original certificates and one set of self-attested photocopies of relevant testimonials, on the date of the interview. Report to CSIR-CECRI, Karaikudi by 08:45 AM on 10 August 2026. Entry will not be permitted after 10:30 AM.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 27 vacancies in total - 18 under Trade (ITI), 5 under Technician (Diploma), and 4 under Graduate (Degree) categories.

faq.question: Q2. What is the stipend for CSIR-CECRI Apprentice Trainee?
faq.answer: The stipend ranges from Rs. 6,800 to Rs. 12,300 per month, depending on the trade, discipline, and category.

faq.question: Q3. When is the CSIR-CECRI Karaikudi walk-in interview?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled for 10 August 2026 (Monday) at 09:00 AM, with a reporting time of 08:45 AM. No candidate will be permitted to enter after 10:30 AM.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for CSIR-CECRI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Trade (ITI) candidates must be at least 14 years old with no upper limit; Technician (Diploma) candidates must be 18–24 years; Graduate (Degree) candidates must be 21–26 years, as on 10 August 2026.

faq.question: Q5. How do I register for CSIR-CECRI apprenticeship?
faq.answer: ITI (Trade) candidates must register at apprenticeshipindia.org/candidate-registration, while Graduate (Degree) and Technician (Diploma) candidates must register at nats.education.gov.in, before appearing for the walk-in interview.

faq.question: Q6. Is CSIR-CECRI apprenticeship a permanent job?
faq.answer: No. Upon completion of the apprenticeship period, CSIR-CECRI has no obligation to provide regular employment, and apprentices have no right to claim regular employment based on completing the apprenticeship.

faq.question: Q7. Are candidates with work experience eligible for CSIR-CECRI Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: No. Candidates with one year or more of work experience in any organization after acquiring the essential qualification are not eligible.
